title: parathelypteris viscosa
intro: Parathelypteris viscosa, also known as sticky fern, is a type of fern native to Asia and Europe, characterized by its distinctive sticky rhizome and fronds. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Parathelypteris viscosa include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying the fronds and rhizome, then using them to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
